title: $occdbmod
text: $occdbmod Return the modification status of a database occurrence. $occdbmod { ( Entity ) } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description Entity String Entity name. If omitted, the current entity is used. Return Values Table 2. Values returned in $occdbmod Value Meaning "" Entity does not exist or is not present in the component structure. 0 In the following cases: No modifications have been made to database fields in the occurrence. No entities are present in the component structure. 1 In the following cases: A field in the occurrence that is defined as being a database field has been modified. The current occurrence is a database occurrence that has been removed. Use Allowed in all component types, except self-contained Reports. Description $occdbmod is an occurrence-level function that tests whether any database field in the current occurrence has been modified. Non-database fields do not affect $occdbmod . Events that cause a field to be modified (and $occdbmod set to 1) include: The user enters a retrieve profile in an empty field. The user changes the value of data that has been retrieved. A non-database field is assigned a new value in ProcScript without the /init switch. (You could consider $occdbmod to be an inclusive logical OR of the values of the $fielddbmod functions for all database fields in the occurrence.) Related Links $fielddbmod Return the modification status of a database field. $fieldmod Return the modification status of a field. $instancemod Return or set the modification status of data in the current component instance. $instancedbmod Return the modification status of database fields in the current component instance. $occmod Return the modification status of an occurrence.
